subject: Statistics
title: The Hawthorne Studies
description: A fast breakdown of The Hawthorne Studies for builders.

hook.text: Factory workers became more productive when researchers watched them. Lighting didn't matter — attention did.

buildup.text: At the Hawthorne Works in the 1920s, every change to working conditions seemed to boost productivity.

discovery.text: The Hawthorne effect: people change behavior when they know they're being observed, regardless of the intervention.

twist.text: Re-analysis revealed coaching, selection bias, and demand effects. The experiment's validity is now disputed.

climax.text: Every experiment with human subjects must account for the fact that observation itself changes behavior.

punchline.text: Watch people and they change. The observer is never truly invisible.

quiz.question: What is the Hawthorne effect?
options: People change their behavior when they know they're being observed
